task_completion.description: Privacy partition walls are drawn to divide the open building shell into multiple functional zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sweet Home 3D 2D plan or 3D view, new interior walls should be visible, dividing the large open space into separate rooms or areas.

task_completion.description: Doors are placed for zone separation and emergency egress.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Door objects from the catalog should be visible in the 2D plan or 3D view, inserted into the newly created partition walls or exterior walls.

task_completion.description: Text labels identifying each functional zone are added to the layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels (e.g., 'Sleeping Dormitory', 'Dining', 'Intake', 'Sanitation', 'Storage') should be visible on the 2D plan view.

task_completion.description: Zones are furnished appropriately, including a high density of beds, chairs, and tables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D plan and 3D view should show a large number of beds (at least 30) in the sleeping dormitory, and many chairs (at least 30) and tables (at least 6) in the communal dining area, along with other appropriate furniture in different zones.

task_completion.description: The completed project is saved to /home/ga/Documents/emergency_shelter.sh3d.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D window title bar should display 'emergency_shelter.sh3d', or a file manager/terminal should show the file saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to design the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the layout being actively edited (drawing walls, dragging furniture), rather than just a script or XML file being modified in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent used only the built-in catalog for furnishing.
integrity.visual_evidence: The furniture catalog pane in Sweet Home 3D should be used; there should be no evidence of downloading or importing external 3D models from the web.

integrity.description: The file was genuinely saved using the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog or the application title bar should reflect the correct file name, rather than a terminal command echoing text into a file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying an empty building shell in Sweet Home 3D. The VLM should verify the creation of interior partition walls, placement of doors/windows, placement of text labels for zoning, and high-density furniture placement (specifically more than 30 beds, more than 30 chairs, and more than 6 tables). The final file must be saved as 'emergency_shelter.sh3d' in the Documents folder.
